title: Area and perimeter situations
description: Determine if a situation represents area or perimeter.

assessment_items.question_content:
Marcie is putting squares onto a rectangular mat. After she finishes covering it, Marcie says the mat measures $30$ square units.

**Which measurement did Marcie find?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Perimeter** is the distance around the outside of a two-dimensional shape.

**Area** is the space inside of a two-dimensional shape. We can also think of area as the amount of space a shape covers.

**Length** is the distance from one point to another.
Which measurement has Marcie found?
Marcie found the amount of square units that **cover** her mat. So, she found the area of the mat.

assessment_items.question_content:
Ginnie plans to paint a wall. She measures its height and width. She finds that she needs enough paint to cover $8$ square meters.

**Which measurement does $8$ square meters represent?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Perimeter** is the distance around the outside of a two-dimensional shape.

**Area** is the space inside of a two-dimensional shape. We can also think of area as the amount of space a shape covers.

**Length** is the distance from one point to another.
Which measurement has Ginnie found?
Ginnie found the amount of square meters that **cover** her wall. So, she found the area.

assessment_items.question_content:
Jack is building a square garden.  Each side length measures $7$ meters.  Jack multiplies $7\times7$ to find the amount of space in his garden is equal to $49$ square meters.

**Which measurement does $49$ square meters represent?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Perimeter** is the distance around the outside of a two-dimensional shape.

**Area** is the space inside of a two-dimensional shape. We can also think of area as the amount of space a shape covers.

**Length** is the distance from one point to another.
Which measurement has Jack found?
Jack found the amount of square meters that **cover** his garden. So, he found the area.

assessment_items.question_content:
Theresa wants new carpeting for her family room. Her family room is a $6$ meter by $10$ meter rectangle. Theresa said she will need $60$ square meters of carpet.

**Which measurement does $60$ square meters represent?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Perimeter** is the distance around the outside of a two-dimensional shape.

**Area** is the space inside of a two-dimensional shape. We can also think of area as the amount of space a shape covers.

**Length** is the distance from one point to another.
Which measurement has Theresa found?
Theresa found the amount of square meters that **cover** her family room floor. So, she found the area.

assessment_items.question_content:
Milo wants to put a fence around the dog park near his house. He measures the sides and finds they are $7\text{ m}$ and $8\text{ m}$ long. He multiplies $7\times2$ and $8\times2$ and finds he needs $30\text{ m}$ of fencing.


**Which measurement does $30\text{ m}$ represent?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Perimeter** is the distance around the outside of a two-dimensional shape.

**Area** is the space inside of a two-dimensional shape. We can also think of area as the amount of space a shape covers.

**Length** is the distance from one point to another.
Which measurement has Milo found?
Milo found the distance **around** the dog park. So, he found the **perimeter**.

assessment_items.question_content:
Before soccer practice, Laura warms up by jogging around the outside of the entire soccer field.  The field measures $80$ meters by $120$ meters. 

**If Laura wants to know how many meters she jogged in all, which measurement should she find?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Perimeter** is the distance around the outside of a two-dimensional shape.

**Area** is the space inside of a two-dimensional shape. We can also think of area as the amount of space a shape covers.

**Length** is the distance from one point to another.
Which measurement would Laura find?
Laura would find the distance **around** the soccer field. So, she would be finding the **perimeter**.

assessment_items.question_content:
Les is measuring the border of her bulletin board. She measures around the entire outside of the bulletin board and finds the distance is $32$ units.


**Which measurement does $32$ units represent?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Perimeter** is the distance around the outside of a two-dimensional shape.

**Area** is the space inside of a two-dimensional shape. We can also think of area as the amount of space a shape covers.

**Length** is the distance from one point to another.
Which measurement has Les found?
Les found the distance **around** her bulletin board. So, she found the **perimeter**.

assessment_items.question_content:
Supriya is decorating her bedroom. She plans to hang strings of twinkle lights all the way around her walls. She measures the walls and finds she will need $36$ meters of lights.


**Which measurement did Supriya find?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Perimeter** is the distance around the outside of a two-dimensional shape.

**Area** is the space inside of a two-dimensional shape. We can also think of area as the amount of space a shape covers.

**Length** is the distance from one point to another.
Which measurement has Supriya found?
Supriya found the distance **around** her bedroom. So, she found the **perimeter**.

assessment_items.question_content:
Ted is building a garden in his yard. He measures one side of his yard and finds it is $12$ meters.  

**Which measurement did Ted find?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Perimeter** is the distance around the outside of a two-dimensional shape.

**Area** is the space inside of a two-dimensional shape. We can also think of area as the amount of space a shape covers.

**Length** is the distance from one point to another.
Which measurement has Ted found?
Ted measured one **side** of his yard So, he found the **length**.

assessment_items.question_content:
Talia is going to put carpet in her new house. She measures one side of her living room and finds it measures $17$ meters.

**Which measurement does $17$ meters represent?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Perimeter** is the distance around the outside of a two-dimensional shape.

**Area** is the space inside of a two-dimensional shape. We can also think of area as the amount of space a shape covers.

**Length** is the distance from one point to another.
Which measurement has Talia found?
Talia measured one **side** of her living room. So, she found the **length**.

assessment_items.question_content:
Jane is in charge of making a banner for the basketball game this
Saturday. She measures how long the banner is before painting it. She finds it measures $10$ meters.

**Which measurement did Jane find?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Perimeter** is the distance around the outside of a two-dimensional shape.

**Area** is the space inside of a two-dimensional shape. We can also think of area as the amount of space a shape covers.

**Length** is the distance from one point to another.
Which measurement has Jane found?
Jane measured one **side** of the banner. So, she found the **length**.

assessment_items.question_content:
Betty wants to put new cabinets in her kitchen. She measures one side of her kitchen and finds it measures $18$ meters.

**Which measurement did Betty find?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Perimeter** is the distance around the outside of a two-dimensional shape.

**Area** is the space inside of a two-dimensional shape. We can also think of area as the amount of space a shape covers.

**Length** is the distance from one point to another.
Which measurement has Betty found?
Betty measured one **side** of her kitchen. So, she found the **length**.
